info.title: GoatCounter API
info.description: Reference documentation for the GoatCounter API. GoatCounter is an open-source, privacy-friendly web analytics platform.

post.description:
This can count one or more pageviews. Pageviews are not persisted
immediately, but persisted in the background every 10 seconds.

The maximum amount of pageviews per request is 500.

Errors will have the key set to the index of the pageview. Any pageviews not
listed have been processed and shouldn't be sent again.

post.summary: Start a new export in the background.
post.description:
This starts a new export in the background; this can only be done once an
hour.

get.description:
The export may take a while to generate, depending on the size. It will
return a 202 Accepted status code if the export ID is still running.

Export files are kept for 24 hours, after which they're deleted. This will
return a 400 Gone status code if the export has been deleted.

get.summary: Get information about the current user and API key.
get.description: Get information about the current user and API key.

get.summary: Get an overview of paths on this site (without statistics).
get.description: Get an overview of paths on this site (without statistics).

parameters.name: Limit
parameters.description: Limit number of returned results

parameters.name: After
parameters.description: Only select paths after this ID, for pagination.

get.description: Get all information about one site.

post.description:
A POST request will *replace* the entire site with what's sent, blanking out
any existing fields that may exist. A PATCH request will only update the
fields that are sent.

patch.description:
A POST request will *replace* the entire site with what's sent, blanking out
any existing fields that may exist. A PATCH request will only update the
fields that are sent.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start time, should be rounded to the hour.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End time, should be rounded to the hour.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of pages to get.

parameters.name: group
parameters.description:
Set Max value in the response to the highest daily, weekly, or
monthly value, instead of hourly.

The Hourly, Daily, Weekly, and Monthly values are always included in
the response – this only affects the Max value, which is useful if
you want to draw charts like the GoatCounter dashboard: you need to
know the maximum Y-axis value of the chart to draw it.

parameters.name: path_by_name
parameters.description:
Get values for include_paths and exclude_paths by path name, rather
than path ID. This is more convenient in some cases, but also a bit
slower.

parameters.name: daily
parameters.description: Deprecated: identical to group=day and will be removed in the future.

parameters.name: include_paths
parameters.description:
Include only these path IDs; default is to include everything.

If path_by_name is set, it will look up paths by name instead of ID.

parameters.name: exclude_paths
parameters.description:
Exclude these path IDs, for pagination.

If path_by_name is set, it will look up paths by name instead of ID.

get.summary: Get an overview of referral information for a path.
get.description: Get an overview of referral information for a path.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start time, should be rounded to the hour.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End time, should be rounded to the hour.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of pages to get.

get.summary: Count total number of pageviews for a date range.
get.description:
This is mostly useful to display things like browser stats as a percentage of
the total; the /api/v0/pages endpoint only counts the pageviews until it's
paginated.

parameters.name: start
parameters.description: Start time, should be rounded to the hour.

parameters.name: end
parameters.description: End time, should be rounded to the hour.

parameters.name: path_by_name
parameters.description:
Get values for include_paths and exclude_paths by path name, rather
than path ID. This is more convenient in some cases, but also a bit
slower.

parameters.name: include_paths
parameters.description:
Include only these path IDs; default is to include everything.

If path_by_name is set, it will look up paths by name instead of ID.

get.description:
Page can be: browsers, systems, locations, languages, sizes, campaigns,
toprefs.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of pages to get.

Get values for include_paths and exclude_paths by path name, rather

parameters.name: include_paths

Include only these path IDs; default is to include everything.

get.summary: Get detailed stats for an ID.
get.description: Page can be: browsers, systems, locations, sizes, campaigns, toprefs.

parameters.name: start

parameters.name: end

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Offset for pagination.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of pages to get.

parameters.name: path_by_name
parameters.description:
Get values for include_paths and exclude_paths by path name, rather
than path ID. This is more convenient in some cases, but also a bit

Include only these path IDs; default is to include everything.

If path_by_name is set, it will look up paths by name instead of ID.

count.description: Number of visitors for the selected date range.

max.description: Highest visitors per hour or day (depending on daily being set).

ref_scheme.description:
What kind of referral this is; only set when retrieving referrals .

 h HTTP Referal header.
 g Generated; for example are Google domains (google.com, google.nl,
 google.co.nz, etc.) are grouped as the generated referral "Google".
 c Campaign (via query parameter)
 o Other

monthly.description:
Visitors for the month; set on first day of the month. This value will
not be set if it's 0.

weekly.description:
Visitors for the week; set once every 7 days. This value will not be set
if it's 0.

id.description: ID for selecting more details; not present in the detail view.

ref_scheme.description:
What kind of referral this is; only set when retrieving referrals .

 h HTTP Referal header.
 g Generated; for example are Google domains (google.com, google.nl,
 google.co.nz, etc.) are grouped as the generated referral "Google".
 c Campaign (via query parameter)
 o Other

cname.description:
Custom domain, e.g. "stats.example.com".

When self-hosting this is the domain/vhost your site is accessible at.

code.description:
Domain code (e.g. "arp242", which makes arp242.goatcounter.com). Only
used for goatcounter.com and not when self-hosting.

link_domain.description:
Site domain for linking (www.arp242.net). Note this can be a full URL and
is a bit misnamed.

received_data.description:
Whether this site has received any data; will be true after the first
pageview.

goatcounter.SiteSettings.title: SiteSettings
goatcounter.SiteSettings.description:
SiteSettings contains all the user-configurable settings for a site, with
the exception of the domain settings.

This is stored as JSON in the database.

last_report_at.description: Keep track when the last email report was sent, so we don't double-send them.

goatcounter.View.title: View
goatcounter.View.description:
Views for the dashboard; these settings apply to all widget and are
configurable in the yellow box at the top.

goatcounter.Widget.title: Widget
goatcounter.Widget.description: Widgets is a list of widgets to be printed, in order.

filter.description:
Filter pageviews; accepted values:

 ip Ignore requests coming from IP addresses listed in "Settings → Ignore IP". Requires the IP field to be set.

["ip"] is used if this field isn't sent; send an empty array ([]) to not
filter anything.

The X-Goatcounter-Filter header will be set to a list of indexes if any
pageviews are filtered; for example:

 X-Goatcounter-Filter: 5, 10

This header will be omitted if nothing is filtered.

hits.description: Hits is the list of pageviews.

no_sessions.description:
By default it's an error to send pageviews that don't have either a
Session or UserAgent and IP set. This avoids accidental errors.

When this is set it will just continue without recording sessions for
pageviews that don't have these parameters set.

bot.description:
Hint if this should be considered a bot; should be one of the JSBot*`
constants from isbot; note the backend may override this if it
detects a bot using another method.
https://github.com/zgoat/isbot/blob/master/isbot.go#L28

created_at.description:
Time this pageview should be recorded at; this can be in the past,
but not in the future.

ip.description:
IP to get location from; not used if location is set. Also used for
session generation.

location.description: Location as ISO-3166-1 alpha2 string (e.g. NL, ID, etc.)

path.description: Path of the pageview, or the event name.

query.description: Query parameters for this pageview, used to get campaign parameters.

ref.description:
Referrer value, can be an URL (i.e. the Referal: header) or any
string.

session.description:
Normally a session is based on hash(User-Agent+IP+salt), but if you don't
send the IP address then we can't determine the session.

In those cases, you can store your own session identifiers and send them
along. Note these will not be stored in the database as the sessionID
(just as the hashes aren't), they're just used as a unique grouping
identifier.

size.description:
Screen width.

For compatibility it also accepts the size as "width,height,scaling", but
the height and scaling are not used and this format is deprecated.

title.description: Page title, or some descriptive event title.

stats.description: Total overview per day and hour.

total_events.description: Total number of visitors for events.

total_utc.description:
Total number of visitors in UTC. The browser, system, etc, stats are
always in UTC.

handlers.apiError.title: apiError
handlers.apiError.description:
Generic API error. An error will have either the "error" or "errors"
field set, but not both.

start_from_hit_id.description: Pagination cursor; only export hits with an ID greater than this.

hits.description: Sorted list of paths with their visitor and pageview count.

total.description: Total number of visitors in the returned result.

more.description: True if there are more paths.

paths.description: List of paths, sorted by ID.

stats.description: Sorted list of paths with their visitor and pageview count.

handlers.authError.title: authError
handlers.authError.description: Authentication error: the API key was not provided or incorrect.

Abbr.description: WITA – the correct abbreviation may change depending on the time of year (i.e. CET and CEST, depending on DST).

Comments.description: Borneo (east, south); Sulawesi/Celebes, Bali, Nusa Tengarra; Timor (west)

error.description: Any errors that may have occured.

last_hit_id.description: Last hit ID that was exported; can be used as start_from_hit_id.

start_from_hit_id.description: The hit ID this export was started from.

ErrorResponse.title: apiError
ErrorResponse.description:
Generic API error. An error will have either the "error" or "errors"
field set, but not both.

BearerAuth.description: API token passed as Bearer token
